repo_prior_art.cite: none; closest is flagship_explore/f1, but that is T=3 full-history/stagewise nonsharpness, not T=2 post-baseline instrument-memory deconvolution for first-period compliers

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest SojitraSyrgkanis2024, https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.01463, identifies dynamic LATEs under one-sided noncompliance and cross-period conditions rather than proving partial-ID strictness from assignment memory

published_prior_art.cite: none; Han2023, https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.2023.2238941, gives IV-based partial welfare ordering for dynamic regimes, not this memory-gain boundary

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite response-type LP representation is routine and subsumed at the published-axis level by generic discrete sharp-bound machinery in DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 and the Balke-Pearl latent-table template.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The sustained-treatment IV comparator SwansonLabrecqueHernan2018, https://doi.org/10.1007/s10654-018-0396-6, is missing; it does not subsume the kernel but is closer than several generic static IV citations for sustained-strategy IV scope.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The Markovized comparison is not a valid constraint-dropping image as stated: after conditioning on post-treatment D1 and dropping Z1, the induced Z2 assignment can depend on latent first-period stratum, so full-history feasible laws need not satisfy a Markovized randomized-assignment model using only Pr(Z2|X,D1).

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Strict containment inherits the ill-posed Markovized benchmark from Theorem 2; the proposal must define MK as a genuine relaxation/projection before strictness is mathematically meaningful.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proposed boundary mentions always-taker/complier mixtures but omits the analogous complier/never-taker mixture in D1=0 cells, which is directly relevant to the Y(0,0) component of the sustained complier contrast.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the Markovized benchmark as an explicit projection/relaxation of the full-history feasible polytope, or allow latent-type-dependent coarsened Z2 propensities induced by marginalizing Z1; then restate containment against that object.
Revise the minimal-boundary conjecture to handle both D1=1 always-taker/complier mixtures and D1=0 never-taker/complier mixtures, with separate boundary clauses for the Y(1,1) and Y(0,0) components.
Add a rational T=2 LP witness with primal feasible distributions and dual certificates showing a strict IM-vs-MK endpoint gap after the benchmark is repaired.

Add Swanson-Labrecque-Hernan 2018 to the related-work/checklist discussion as the closest sustained-treatment IV null-testing comparator, and explain why null-testability is orthogonal to sharp sustained-effect bounds.

recommended_next_step: Reject this draft as stated; redraft the Markovized benchmark and boundary conditions before another novelty pass.
